i: MIREMPET (Ministério dos Recursos Minerais, Petróleo e Gás) — Angola's ministry overseeing oil and gas operations, licensing, and regulation. ANPG (Agência Nacional de Petróleo, Gás e Biocombustíveis) — the national petroleum regulator, created in 2019 to replace Sonangol's dual role as regulator and operator. Lei 3/20 (Law on Public Probity) — Angola's anti-corruption law establishing asset declaration requirements and conflicts of interest rules for public officials. Whistleblower protection remains weak.

Sonangol E.P. — Angola's state oil company and the country's largest enterprise. Sonangol holds equity in all oil production sharing agreements and manages the state's petroleum interests. The company underwent restructuring in 2019, separating its regulatory functions to ANPG. TotalEnergies, BP, ExxonMobil, Eni — major international oil companies operating deepwater blocks offshore Angola.

ANPG (Agência Nacional de Petróleo, Gás e Biocombustíveis) — established in 2019 as the independent petroleum regulator, responsible for managing exploration and production contracts, monitoring compliance with license terms, and administering bidding rounds. Ministério do Ambiente — Angola's environment ministry, responsible for 환경 policy, impact assessments, and pollution control.

Assembleia Nacional (National Assembly of Angola) — unicameral legislature with 220 members. Angola's electoral system uses party-list proportional representation. The most recent elections were held in 2022. Citizens can engage through assembleia.ao. Angola is a presidential republic with significant executive power. EITI — Angola was suspended from EITI in 2017 for insufficient progress but successfully reapplied and was readmitted in 2020, committing to enhanced 투명성 standards.

AR.s: G20 경제 ($638.4B GDP) with massive Vaca Muerta shale formation (second-largest shale gas, fourth-largest shale oil reserves globally). Agricultural powerhouse (soy, beef) with 5.8% ag GDP share but outsized 환경 footprint. Chronic macroeconomic instability.

sectors.w: Argentina's Vaca Muerta development requires massive capital investment ($10B+/year) and government subsidies (Plan Gas). Removing subsidies breaks the 중복 이익 — without price guarantees, many Vaca Muerta wells are marginal at global market prices.
bp: Phase out Plan Gas subsidies and redirect capital to 재생에너지. Argentina has world-class wind resources in Patagonia and solar in the northwest. ...할 때 renewable electricity undercuts subsidized gas, the 중복 이익 between producers and government in continued fossil expansion breaks.

Mandatory per-well 배출 disclosure including methane venting and flaring. Argentina's Secretaría de Energía already collects production data — extending to 배출 makes W computable from production statistics.
파괴 불가능 by policy alone. Shale gas extraction and combustion release CO₂ and methane by physical process.
a: Report 환경 violations at Vaca Muerta operations to the provincial 환경 당국 (Secretaría de Ambiente de Neuquén)
Document water contamination from fracking operations — report to AIC (Autoridad Interjurisdiccional de las Cuencas)
Report safety violations to the Secretaría de Trabajo
YPF: 개발하다 Paris-aligned transition plan with binding 배출 reduction targets for Vaca Muerta operations
이행하다 TCFD-aligned climate disclosure across all operations
에 투자하다 CCUS (carbon capture, utilization, and storage) pilot projects for gas processing facilities
Secretaría de Energía: Publish per-well 배출 data for all Vaca Muerta operations
SAyDS: 강화하다 federal 환경 standards for unconventional hydrocarbon extraction
ENARGAS: 개혁하다 natural gas tariff structure to eliminate implicit fossil fuel subsidies
귀하의 대표에게 연락하십시오: Diputado Nacional or Senador 을 요구하기 위해 a timeline for fossil fuel subsidy phase-out and transparent per-well 배출 reporting
강화하다 Argentina's NDC — current targets are considered insufficient by Climate Action Tracker
Leverage Mercosur-EU trade agreement 환경 provisions to align energy transition commitments
Support South American 재생에너지 integration through regional grid interconnection
i: Secretaría de Ambiente de Neuquén — Neuquén province's 환경 당국, responsible for 환경 permitting and monitoring of oil and gas operations in the Vaca Muerta formation. AIC (Autoridad Interjurisdiccional de las Cuencas de los ríos Limay, Neuquén y Negro) — the inter-jurisdictional water authority managing the river basins affected by Vaca Muerta operations. COFEMA (Consejo Federal de Medio Ambiente) — federal-provincial 환경 coordination body. Argentina lacks comprehensive federal 내부고발자 protection legislation, though provincial laws vary.

YPF S.A. — Argentina's largest energy company (51% 국영), the dominant operator in Vaca Muerta. YPF's strategic decisions on Vaca Muerta development drive national energy policy. Pan American Energy, Vista Energy, TotalEnergies — other major operators in Vaca Muerta. Plan Gas.Ar — the Argentine government's gas production incentive program, guaranteeing minimum prices for domestic gas production to stimulate Vaca Muerta development.

Secretaría de Energía — Argentina's energy secretariat, responsible for energy policy, production monitoring, and regulatory oversight. SAyDS (Secretaría de Ambiente y Desarrollo Sustentable) — the federal environment secretariat, responsible for 환경 policy and 기후변화. ENARGAS (Ente Nacional Regulador del Gas) — the national gas regulatory authority, overseeing gas transport, distribution, and tariff regulation.

Congreso de la Nación Argentina — bicameral legislature consisting of the Cámara de Diputados (257 members) and the Senado (72 members). Citizens can contact representatives through diputados.gov.ar and senado.gob.ar. The Comisión de Energía y Combustibles handles energy legislation. Argentina's NDC — Argentina submitted an updated NDC in 2021 targeting 배출 of no more than 349 MtCO₂e by 2030. Climate Action Tracker rates this as 'highly insufficient.'

AT.s: Wealthy EU 경제 ($534.8B GDP) with strong manufacturing (15.1% of GDP). Low direct fossil fuel exposure but significant UPF and financial sector PST. Major transit hub for Russian gas (Baumgarten hub). High healthcare expenditure (11.83% GDP) suggests diet-related disease burden.

sectors.w: Austria's healthcare system (ASVG social insurance) tracks diet-related disease costs with high granularity. ...할 때 per-product-category healthcare costs are published alongside retail revenue, W가 계산 가능해진다 from bilateral UPF transactions.
bp: 이행하다 front-of-pack health-impact labelling through EU Farm to Fork Strategy. ...할 때 consumers see welfare costs at point of sale, the 중복 이익 between UPF manufacturers and retailers in high-margin processed products 약화된다.

Mandate publication of diet-attributable healthcare costs per product category using ASVG social insurance data. Austria's comprehensive health insurance system generates the data needed to make W computable.
Not fully breakable. Ultra-processing alters food matrix in ways that affect metabolic response. 개혁하다ulation mandates can reduce but not eliminate 시스템 의존성.
a: Report misleading health claims on food products to the Bundeswettbewerbsbehörde (Federal Competition Authority)
Document undisclosed processing methods — report to AGES (Austrian Agency for Health and Food Safety)
Use the HinweisgeberInnenschutzgesetz (Whistleblower Protection Act, 2023) for protected reporting
개혁하다ulate product lines to reduce ultra-processing — invest in minimally processed alternatives
Voluntarily adopt front-of-pack nutritional labelling ahead of EU mandate
Commission health-impact assessment of product portfolio using national health insurance data

AGES: Publish annual diet-attributable disease burden data linked to specific product categories

Support EU-wide adoption of front-of-pack nutritional labelling
Advocate for inclusion of diet-attributable health costs in national accounting

i: Bundeswettbewerbsbehörde (BWB / Federal Competition Authority) — Austria's competition authority, investigating unfair commercial practices including misleading advertising. AGES (Agentur für Gesundheit und Ernährungssicherheit / Austrian Agency for Health and Food Safety) — the federal agency responsible for food safety, nutrition surveillance, and public health. AGES conducts food inspections and risk assessments. HinweisgeberInnenschutzgesetz (HSchG, 2023) — Austria's transposition of the EU Whistleblower Directive, providing protection for employees reporting violations of EU or national law.

EU Farm to Fork Strategy — includes proposals for mandatory front-of-pack nutrition labelling across the EU. Austrian food companies that move early gain competitive advantage in the EU single market. EFSA (European Food Safety Authority) — provides scientific opinions that shape EU food regulation applicable in Austria.

BMSGPK (Bundesministerium für Soziales, Gesundheit, Pflege und Konsumentenschutz / Federal Ministry of Social Affairs, Health, Care and Consumer Protection) — oversees food safety policy, nutrition guidelines, and consumer protection. Hauptverband der österreichischen Sozialversicherungsträger (now Dachverband) — the umbrella organization of Austrian social insurance institutions, managing healthcare data for the entire insured population.

Österreichisches Parlament — Austria's bicameral parliament consisting of the Nationalrat (183 members) and the Bundesrat (61 members). Citizens can contact representatives through parlament.gv.at. The Gesundheitsausschuss (Health Committee) handles health and food policy legislation.

AZ.s: Oil-dependent 경제 with 29.57% combined oil and gas rents ($22B). SOCAR (State Oil Company) dominates. Hosted COP29 (2024). Authoritarian governance with limited 시민 사회 space. Caspian Sea oil and gas fields (ACG, Shah Deniz) are mature.

sectors.w: Azerbaijan's oil production is declining from mature Caspian fields (ACG peaked in 2010). As production costs rise and reserves deplete, the 중복 이익 between SOCAR and international partners (BP) 약화된다 — diversification becomes economically necessary.
bp: Accelerate post-oil diversification using SOFAZ (State Oil Fund) reserves. Azerbaijan's $50B+ sovereign wealth fund can finance 재생에너지 and non-oil economic development. ...할 때 SOFAZ investment returns from renewables exceed declining oil returns, the 중복 이익 breaks.
이행하다 유전별 배출 disclosure for all Caspian operations. BP already publishes 배출 data for ACG — extending to all SOCAR operations makes W computable across the sector.
파괴 불가능 by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report 환경 violations at oil facilities to the Ministry of Ecology and Natural Resources
Document 배출 underreporting through internal SOCAR channels
Report 환경 concerns to international partners (BP) through their compliance systems
SOCAR: 개발하다 binding 재생에너지 transition plan with interim 배출 targets
BP Azerbaijan: Extend ACG 배출 투명성 standards to all partner operations
SOFAZ: 채택하다 ESG investment criteria excluding new fossil fuel development
Ministry of Ecology: 강화하다 환경 standards for offshore Caspian operations
EITI Azerbaijan: Expand reporting to include 환경 impact data per production block

Leverage Azerbaijan's COP29 presidency legacy to strengthen national climate commitments

i: Ministry of Ecology and Natural Resources — Azerbaijan's 환경 당국, responsible for 환경 monitoring, permitting, and enforcement. The Ministry oversees 환경 impact assessments for energy projects. SOCAR (State Oil Company of the Azerbaijan Republic) — the national oil company controlling all upstream and downstream petroleum operations. Note: Azerbaijan has no comprehensive 내부고발자 protection law. Civil society space is severely constrained; independent media and NGOs face significant operational barriers.

SOCAR — Azerbaijan's state oil company, operating the country's refining, petrochemical, and upstream assets. SOCAR also has international operations in Turkey, Georgia, and Europe. BP — the largest international operator in Azerbaijan, managing the Azeri-Chirag-Deepwater Gunashli (ACG) oilfield and Shah Deniz gas field through Production Sharing Agreements. SOFAZ (State Oil Fund of the Republic of Azerbaijan) — the sovereign wealth fund managing Azerbaijan's petroleum revenues, with assets exceeding $50B.

Azerbaijan EITI — Azerbaijan was an early EITI member (2003) and publishes annual reports on extractive industry revenues. The EITI process provides one of the few independent 투명성 mechanisms in the sector. Tariff (Price) Council — sets regulated energy prices in Azerbaijan. Tehran Convention — the Framework Convention for the Protection of the Marine Environment of the Caspian Sea, which governs 환경 standards for offshore operations.

Milli Majlis (National Assembly of Azerbaijan) — unicameral parliament with 125 members. Note: Azerbaijan is rated 'Not Free' by Freedom House; elections are not considered free and fair by international observers. Civic engagement channels are limited. International pressure operates primarily through multilateral forums including the Council of Europe (Azerbaijan is a member), OSCE, and the EITI Board. The EU-Azerbaijan Partnership and Cooperation Agreement provides a framework for dialogue on energy and 환경 issues.

BA.s: Post-conflict 경제 ($29.6B GDP) with complex governance (two entities: Federation and Republika Srpska). Coal-dependent energy sector and manufacturing (12.7% GDP). EU 후보국 country. Significant coal 광업 and thermal power generation.

sectors.w: Bosnia's coal sector is heavily subsidized through below-market electricity pricing and state employment guarantees. EU accession requires alignment with the EU ETS — when carbon pricing applies, the 중복 이익 between coal producers and the state employment system breaks.
bp: EU accession alignment with EU ETS carbon pricing. ...할 때 Bosnian coal power must pay the carbon price (~€90/ton), coal-fired electricity becomes uncompetitive with imported renewables. The 중복 이익 between mine operators and state employment programs breaks.
Mandatory 배출 monitoring and disclosure per power plant. Energy Community Treaty membership requires EU-aligned 환경 reporting — implementing this makes W computable from electricity generation data.
파괴 불가능. Coal combustion releases CO₂, SO₂, and particulate matter by physical process.
a: Report 환경 violations at coal mines and power plants to entity-level 환경 inspectorates
Document health impacts in communities near coal power plants — report to public health institutes
Report corruption in coal procurement to the Agency for Prevention of Corruption and Coordination of the Fight Against Corruption
State electricity companies (EPBiH, ERS): 개발하다 coal phase-out timelines aligned with Energy Community Treaty obligations
에 투자하다 재생에너지 capacity to replace aging coal plants
이행하다 배출 monitoring per facility ahead of EU ETS alignment
DERK (State Electricity Regulatory Commission): 개혁하다 tariffs to reflect true 환경 costs of coal generation

귀하의 대표에게 연락하십시오: member of the Parlamentarna skupština 을 요구하기 위해 coal phase-out timelines aligned with Energy Community obligations
Support EU accession negotiations contingent on energy transition commitments
Advocate for just transition funding for coal-dependent communities through IPA III (EU Instrument for Pre-Accession)
을 추진: Energy Community Treaty enforcement on air quality standards
i: Environmental inspection is split between the Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina and Republika Srpska, each maintaining separate 환경 agencies. Federation: Federalni inspektorat — 환경 inspectorate. Republika Srpska: Republička uprava za inspekcijske poslove — inspection administration. Agency for Prevention of Corruption and Coordination of the Fight Against Corruption (APIK) — state-level anti-corruption body. Bosnia lacks comprehensive 내부고발자 protection legislation, though draft laws have been discussed as part of EU accession reforms.

EPBiH (Elektroprivreda Bosne i Hercegovine) — the Federation's state electricity company, operating coal-fired power plants and hydropower. ERS (Elektroprivreda Republike Srpske) — Republika Srpska's state electricity company. Both companies operate aging coal infrastructure (many plants dating to Yugoslav era) and are major employers in 광업 communities.

DERK (Državna regulatorna komisija za električnu energiju / State Electricity Regulatory Commission) — the state-level electricity regulator. FERK and RERS are entity-level regulators. Energy Community Treaty — Bosnia is a contracting party, committed to implementing EU energy and 환경 acquis including Large Combustion Plants Directive and Industrial Emissions Directive.

Parlamentarna skupština Bosne i Hercegovine (Parliamentary Assembly) — bicameral legislature consisting of the Predstavnički dom (House of Representatives, 42 members) and the Dom naroda (House of Peoples, 15 members). Bosnia's complex governance structure (Dayton Agreement) divides power between state, entity, and cantonal levels. Citizens can contact representatives through parlament.ba.

BD.s: Large 경제 ($450.1B GDP) driven by garment manufacturing (21.9% mfg share of GDP). High agricultural employment (11.2% GDP). Extreme climate vulnerability — low-lying delta at severe flood and cyclone risk. One of the world's most densely populated countries.

sectors.w: Bangladesh's rice monoculture depletes groundwater (arsenic contamination) and reduces soil biodiversity. Satellite-based crop monitoring and water quality data from BWDB can make W computable from agricultural output and water extraction volumes.
bp: 개혁하다 agricultural subsidies from input-based (fertilizer, diesel) to outcome-based (soil health, water efficiency). ...할 때 subsidies reward diversification rather than rice intensification, ...에 대한 중복 이익이 monoculture between farmers and input suppliers 약화된다.

Mandatory groundwater extraction monitoring and arsenic contamination mapping published at upazila level. BWDB already collects water data — extending to public disclosure linked to agricultural output makes W computable.

부분적으로 파괴 가능. Intensive rice cultivation in deltaic conditions inherently alters hydrology and nutrient cycling. However, System of Rice Intensification (SRI) and crop diversification can reduce 시스템 의존성.
a: Report pesticide misuse or adulteration to DAE (Department of Agricultural Extension)
Document groundwater over-extraction or arsenic contamination — report to DPHE (Department of Public Health Engineering)
Report food safety violations in agricultural supply chains to BFSA (Bangladesh Food Safety Authority)

에 투자하다 climate-resilient crop varieties through partnerships with BRRI (Bangladesh Rice Research Institute)
채택하다 water-use efficiency targets across supply chains
Ministry of Agriculture: 개혁하다 subsidy structure from input-based to outcome-based
BWDB: 이행하다 mandatory groundwater monitoring networks in intensive agricultural zones
DoE (Department of Environment): 집행하다 regulations on agricultural chemical runoff into waterways
귀하의 대표에게 연락하십시오: member of the Jatiya Sangsad 을 요구하기 위해 reform of agricultural subsidies toward climate resilience and crop diversification
Support Bangladesh's Climate Vulnerable Forum leadership in advocating for agricultural adaptation finance
Advocate for inclusion of groundwater depletion costs in national GDP accounting
을 추진: international climate finance to support agricultural diversification away from rice monoculture
i: DAE (Department of Agricultural Extension) — the primary government agency for agricultural technical support and extension services, operating at union, upazila, and district levels. DPHE (Department of Public Health Engineering) — responsible for water supply and sanitation, including monitoring arsenic contamination in groundwater (affecting ~35 million people). BFSA (Bangladesh Food Safety Authority) — established in 2015 to regulate food safety across the supply chain. Bangladesh lacks comprehensive 내부고발자 protection legislation.

BRRI (Bangladesh Rice Research Institute) — the national research institute developing improved rice varieties, including flood-tolerant (BRRI dhan52), salt-tolerant, and drought-resistant varieties. BARI (Bangladesh Agricultural Research Institute) — conducts research on non-rice crops including vegetables, pulses, and oilseeds. BADC (Bangladesh Agricultural 개발하다ment Corporation) — the government agency responsible for seed production and distribution.

Ministry of Agriculture — oversees agricultural policy, subsidies, and development programs. BWDB (Bangladesh Water 개발하다ment Board) — responsible for flood control, drainage, and irrigation infrastructure. The BWDB manages the country's water resource data system. DoE (Department of Environment) — Bangladesh's 환경 regulatory agency, responsible for 환경 clearance of projects and monitoring pollution.

Jatiya Sangsad (National Parliament of Bangladesh) — unicameral legislature with 350 members (300 directly elected, 50 reserved for women). Citizens can contact representatives through parliament.gov.bd. The Standing Committee on Ministry of Agriculture handles agricultural policy. Bangladesh Climate Change Strategy and Action Plan (BCCSAP) — the national framework for climate adaptation and mitigation, including agricultural resilience.

BE.s: EU and NATO headquarters 경제 ($671.4B GDP). Services-dominant (71% GDP). Major port (Antwerp — Europe's second-largest) handles significant fossil fuel and petrochemical throughput. High healthcare expenditure (10.8% GDP). Federal state with three regions.

sectors.w: Belgium's comprehensive INAMI/RIZIV health insurance system tracks healthcare costs with high granularity. Publishing diet-attributable disease costs per product category makes W computable from retail sales data.
bp: 이행하다 front-of-pack health labelling (Belgium adopted Nutri-Score voluntarily in 2019). Extending to mandatory application and adding health-cost information 약화된다 ...에 대한 중복 이익이 high-margin UPF.
INAMI/RIZIV publication of anonymized diet-attributable healthcare cost data per product category. Belgium's universal health insurance generates comprehensive data to make W computable.
Not fully breakable. Ultra-processing alters food matrix affecting metabolic response. 개혁하다ulation mandates reduce but do not eliminate 시스템 의존성.

Document food safety violations — report to AFSCA/FAVV (Federal Agency for the Safety of the Food Chain)
Use the Belgian 내부고발자 protection law (transposition of EU Directive 2019/1937) for protected reporting

귀하의 대표에게 연락하십시오: member of the Chambre des représentants / Kamer van Volksvertegenwoordigers 을 요구하기 위해 mandatory health-impact labelling
Support EU-wide mandatory Nutri-Score through the Farm to Fork Strategy

i: AFSCA/FAVV (Agence fédérale pour la sécurité de la chaîne alimentaire / Federaal Agentschap voor de Veiligheid van de Voedselketen) — Belgium's federal food safety agency, responsible for food chain safety from farm to fork. Conducts inspections, sampling, and enforcement. SPF Économie (Service public fédéral Économie) — the federal economic affairs service handling consumer protection and fair commercial practices.

Fevia (Federation of the Belgian Food Industry) — the trade association representing over 700 food companies in Belgium. Fevia's sustainability commitments influence industry practices. Belgium hosts major food companies including Puratos, Vandemoortele, and Lotus Bakeries.

SPF Santé publique (Federal Public Service Health, Food Chain Safety and Environment) — the federal ministry overseeing public health policy, food safety, and environment. INAMI/RIZIV (Institut national d'assurance maladie-invalidité / Rijksinstituut voor ziekte- en invaliditeitsverzekering) — Belgium's national health insurance institute, managing the compulsory health insurance system and healthcare cost data.

Parlement fédéral / Federaal Parlement — Belgium's bicameral parliament consisting of the Chambre des représentants / Kamer van Volksvertegenwoordigers (150 members) and the Sénat / Senaat (60 members). Citizens can contact representatives through lachambre.be / dekamer.be. Belgium's federal structure means health policy is shared between federal and regional (community) governments.

BF.s: Low-income Sahelian 경제 ($23.1B GDP) with dominant gold 광업 sector (min=15.45% GDP). Agriculture employs 80%+ of population (20.9% GDP). Military junta since 2022 coup. Severe security challenges (jihadist insurgency).

sectors.w: Burkina Faso's gold 광업 generates cyanide contamination, mercury pollution (artisanal 광업), and community displacement. ...할 때 환경 and health costs per mine are published alongside production revenue, W가 계산 가능해진다.
bp: Mandate 환경 bonds and community benefit agreements for all industrial 광업 licenses. ...할 때 광업 companies must pre-fund 환경 remediation and community compensation, ...에 대한 중복 이익이 extraction without accountability 약화된다.

Publish 광산별 환경 impact data (water quality, air quality, land disturbance) alongside production revenue through EITI reporting. Burkina Faso's EITI membership provides the framework — extending to 환경 data makes W visible.
부분적으로 파괴 가능. Open-pit gold 광업 inherently disturbs landscapes and generates toxic waste. However, improved tailings management and mercury-free artisanal methods can reduce 시스템 의존성.
a: Report 환경 violations at mine sites to BUNEE (Bureau National des Évaluations Environnementales)
Document community health impacts near 광업 operations through local health clinics
Report illegal artisanal 광업 activities and mercury use to provincial authorities
Mining companies (Endeavour Mining, IAMGOLD, Nordgold): 이행하다 Global Industry Standard on Tailings Management
채택하다 EITI enhanced reporting including 환경 impact metrics
에 투자하다 mercury-free processing technologies for artisanal 광업 support programs
Ministry of Mines: 강화하다 환경 conditions in 광업 permits and enforce compliance
BUMIGEB (Bureau des Mines et de la Géologie): Improve artisanal 광업 regulation to reduce mercury use

Support Kimberley Process-style certification for gold supply chains to reduce conflict mineral financing
i: BUNEE (Bureau National des Évaluations Environnementales) — Burkina Faso's national 환경 assessment authority, responsible for 환경 impact assessments of 광업 projects. Ministère de l'Environnement, de l'Énergie, de l'Eau et de l'Assainissement — oversees 환경 policy and regulation. Note: Under military rule since 2022, institutional capacity is constrained and 시민 사회 space is restricted.

Major 광업 companies operating in Burkina Faso include Endeavour Mining (Houndé, Wahgnion mines), IAMGOLD (Essakane mine), and Nordgold (Bissa-Bouly). These international companies are subject to home-country ESG reporting requirements and international 광업 standards (ICMM, IFC Performance Standards).

Ministère des Mines et des Carrières — the ministry responsible for 광업 policy, licensing, and regulation. BUMIGEB (Bureau des Mines et de la Géologie du Burkina) — the geological survey and 광업 technical agency. EITI Burkina Faso — Burkina Faso has been an EITI member since 2009, publishing reports on 광업 revenues. The 2015 Mining Code establishes 환경 obligations for 광업 companies.

Note: Burkina Faso is under military rule (Capitaine Ibrahim Traoré since 2022). The Assemblée Législative de Transition (Transitional Legislative Assembly) replaced the elected National Assembly. Democratic institutions are suspended. International engagement channels: ECOWAS (Economic Community of West African States — though Burkina Faso announced withdrawal in 2024), African Union, and international development partners. The African Mining Vision (2009) provides a continental framework for sustainable 광업 governance.

BG.s: EU 회원국 경제 ($113.3B GDP) with legacy coal dependence (Maritsa East complex is one of Europe's largest lignite basins). Services-dominant (63.8% GDP). EU accession has driven institutional modernization but coal transition remains politically sensitive.

sectors.w: Bulgaria's coal sector is sustained by implicit subsidies (below-market electricity pricing, state employment). EU ETS carbon pricing makes lignite uncompetitive — when full carbon costs are internalized, the 중복 이익 between mine operators and state employment programs breaks.
bp: Full alignment with EU ETS free allowance phase-out. ...할 때 Bulgarian lignite plants pay the EU carbon price without free allowances, coal-fired electricity becomes uncompetitive. The 중복 이익 breaks when renewable alternatives are cheaper.

Mandatory 발전소별 배출 and health-impact disclosure. Bulgaria's lignite plants (Maritsa East I, II, III) are among Europe's worst air polluters. Publishing health costs per MWh makes W computable from electricity generation data.
파괴 불가능. Lignite combustion releases CO₂, SO₂, NOx, and particulate matter by physical process.
a: Report 배출 violations at coal plants to RIOSV (Regional Inspectorates of Environment and Water)
Document health impacts in communities near Maritsa East complex — report to RZI (Regional Health Inspectorates)
Use Bulgaria's 내부고발자 protection law (transposition of EU Directive 2019/1937) for protected reporting

에 투자하다 재생에너지 capacity (Bulgaria has strong solar potential — 1,500+ kWh/m²/year)

Support EU Just Transition Fund allocation for Stara Zagora and other coal-dependent regions
Advocate for accelerated EU ETS free allowance phase-out

i: RIOSV (Регионални инспекции по околната среда и водите / Regional Inspectorates of Environment and Water) — 16 regional 환경 agencies conducting inspections and enforcement. EEA (Изпълнителна агенция по околна среда / Executive Environment Agency) — the national 환경 monitoring agency collecting 배출 and air quality data. Bulgaria transposed the EU Whistleblower Directive in 2023.

Maritsa East complex — comprises three lignite-fired power plants (Maritsa East I, II, III) and the associated Maritsa-Iztok mines. AES Bulgaria operates Maritsa East I; ContourGlobal operates Maritsa East III. The complex is the largest industrial employer in the Stara Zagora region. Mini Maritsa-Iztok — the 국영 lignite 광업 company supplying the Maritsa East plants.

KEVR (Комисия за енергийно и водно регулиране / Energy and Water Regulatory Commission) — Bulgaria's independent 에너지 규제기관, setting electricity tariffs and licensing. Ministry of Energy — oversees energy policy including the National Energy and Climate Plan (NECP). Bulgaria's NECP has been criticized for insufficient coal phase-out ambition.

Narodno Sabranie (Народно събрание / National Assembly of Bulgaria) — unicameral parliament with 240 members. Citizens can contact representatives through parliament.bg. The Комисия по енергетика (Energy Committee) handles energy legislation. EU Just Transition Fund — Bulgaria is eligible for significant JTF funding to support coal transition in affected regions. Bulgaria's Territorial Just Transition Plans cover Stara Zagora, Kyustendil, and Pernik regions.

BH.s: Small island petrostate ($47.1B GDP) with 16.64% combined oil and gas rents. Bahrain's Abu Safa offshore field (shared with Saudi Arabia) provides the majority of oil revenue. Diversifying into financial services (Bahrain Financial Harbour) and aluminum (Alba smelter).

sectors.w: Bahrain's oil reserves are the smallest among GCC states (declining production from mature fields). The economic case for diversification is strongest here — when post-oil planning makes renewable investment more attractive than marginal barrel extraction, the 중복 이익 breaks.
bp: Accelerate Economic Vision 2030 diversification away from oil dependence. Bahrain's small size and advanced financial sector make it the most transition-ready GCC 경제. ...할 때 financial services and technology revenue exceeds oil revenue, ...에 대한 중복 이익이 extraction breaks.
Publish 유전별 환경 and 배출 data for all Bahrain Petroleum Company (BAPCO) operations. Making lifecycle 배출 visible per barrel makes W computable from production data.
파괴 불가능 by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report 환경 violations at BAPCO facilities to the Supreme Council for Environment
Document 배출 or safety concerns through internal BAPCO compliance channels
Report concerns to Bahrain's National Audit Office regarding mismanagement of petroleum revenues

i: Supreme Council for Environment (SCE) — Bahrain's 환경 당국, responsible for 환경 policy, permitting, and enforcement. SCE oversees 환경 impact assessments for industrial projects. BAPCO (Bahrain Petroleum Company) — the 국영 oil company operating Bahrain's refinery and upstream assets. National Audit Office — conducts financial audits of government entities including BAPCO. Note: Bahrain has limited 내부고발자 protection; 시민 사회 space is constrained.

BAPCO (Bahrain Petroleum Company) — operates the Sitra refinery (267,000 bpd after modernization) and manages upstream production. Mumtalakat — Bahrain's sovereign wealth fund, managing state assets including BAPCO and Alba (Aluminium Bahrain). Nogaholding — the investment arm of Bahrain's National Oil and Gas Authority, overseeing the country's energy sector investments.

NOGA (National Oil and Gas Authority) — the government body responsible for oil and gas policy, licensing, and upstream regulation. CBB (Central Bank of Bahrain) — the financial sector regulator, which has begun integrating ESG considerations into banking supervision. Bahrain Economic 개발하다ment Board — coordinates the Economic Vision 2030 diversification strategy.

Council of Representatives (Majlis al-Nuwab) — the elected lower house of Bahrain's parliament (40 members) and the Shura Council (Majlis al-Shura, 40 appointed members). Note: Bahrain is a 입헌군주제 with significant royal authority. Political opposition is restricted. The Council of Representatives has limited legislative power. International engagement on climate issues operates through GCC coordination and UNFCCC processes.

BJ.s: Small West African 경제 ($21.5B GDP) with dominant agriculture sector (24.2% GDP, primarily cotton). Services growing (48.9% GDP). Cotton is the main export crop — Benin is Africa's largest cotton producer. Climate vulnerability through Sahel desertification and coastal erosion.

sectors.w: Benin's cotton monoculture degrades soil fertility and increases pesticide exposure. ...할 때 soil health data and pesticide-related healthcare costs are published alongside cotton production revenue, W가 계산 가능해진다 from agricultural output data.
bp: 개혁하다 cotton subsidies to reward soil health and crop diversification. ...할 때 input subsidies (seeds, fertilizer, pesticides) are redirected from cotton intensification to mixed farming systems, the 중복 이익 between cotton traders and input suppliers 약화된다.
Mandatory publication of soil health metrics (organic matter, nutrient balance) and pesticide-related health costs per cotton-producing commune. Making 환경 and health costs visible makes W computable.

부분적으로 파괴 가능. Cotton monoculture inherently depletes soil nutrients and requires pesticide application. However, integrated pest management and organic cotton production can reduce 시스템 의존성 — Benin has a growing organic cotton sector.
a: Report pesticide misuse or adulteration to DPQC (Direction de la Promotion de la Qualité et du Conditionnement des Produits Agricoles)
Document health impacts of pesticide exposure in cotton-growing communities — report to local health centers
Report corruption in cotton sector management to the Front des Organisations Nationales contre la Corruption (FONAC)

에 투자하다 organic cotton certification and premium markets

i: DPQC (Direction de la Promotion de la Qualité et du Conditionnement des Produits Agricoles) — the government directorate responsible for agricultural product quality and standards. ABE (Agence Béninoise pour l'Environnement) — Benin's 환경 agency, responsible for 환경 impact assessment and monitoring. FONAC (Front des Organisations Nationales contre la Corruption) — a national anti-corruption coalition of 시민 사회 organizations.

AIC (Association Interprofessionnelle du Coton) — the inter-professional cotton association coordinating relations between producers, ginners, and input suppliers. SODECO (Société de Développement du Coton) — the main cotton ginning company. The cotton sector was partially liberalized but remains state-coordinated through the AIC framework.

MAEP (Ministère de l'Agriculture, de l'Élevage et de la Pêche) — Benin's agriculture ministry, responsible for agricultural policy and cotton sector coordination. ABE (Agence Béninoise pour l'Environnement) — conducts 환경 monitoring and impact assessments. INSAE (Institut National de la Statistique et de l'Analyse Économique) — Benin's national statistics institute.

Assemblée Nationale du Bénin — unicameral parliament with 109 members. Citizens can contact their deputies through the official Assembly website. Note: Benin's democratic space has narrowed under recent constitutional reforms, though elections continue. Cotton-4 group — Benin, Burkina Faso, Chad, and Mali coordinate on cotton trade policy at the WTO, advocating against subsidies in developed countries that depress global cotton prices.

BN.s: Small wealthy petrostate ($15.3B GDP) with 24.23% combined oil and gas rents. Brunei Shell Petroleum (BSP, 50/50 JV with Shell) dominates the 경제. Absolute monarchy. Wawasan Brunei 2035 diversification strategy underway but oil/gas remain 60%+ of government revenue.

sectors.w: Brunei's oil production is in long-term decline (from 220,000 bpd peak to ~100,000 bpd). As reserves deplete, the 중복 이익 between BSP and the government in continued extraction 약화된다 — Wawasan 2035 diversification becomes economically necessary.
bp: Accelerate Wawasan 2035 economic diversification using Brunei Investment Agency (BIA) sovereign wealth. ...할 때 non-oil sectors generate more government revenue than declining oil production, ...에 대한 중복 이익이 extraction breaks.
이행하다 유전별 배출 disclosure for all BSP and Total operations. Shell's global ESG reporting standards can be extended to Brunei operations to make W computable.
파괴 불가능 by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.

i: BSP (Brunei Shell Petroleum) — a 50/50 joint venture between the government of Brunei and Shell, the country's largest oil and gas producer. BSP operates onshore and offshore fields. Department of Environment, Parks and Recreation — under the Ministry of 개발하다ment, responsible for 환경 policy and regulation. Note: Brunei is an absolute monarchy with no elected legislature. There is no 내부고발자 protection legislation. Reporting channels are primarily internal corporate mechanisms.

BSP (Brunei Shell Petroleum) — managed jointly by the Brunei government and Shell. BIA (Brunei Investment Agency) — the sovereign wealth fund managing Brunei's petroleum revenues (estimated $30-40B). Total E&P Borneo — operates deep water gas fields offshore Brunei. PetroleumBRUNEI — the national petroleum authority overseeing the sector.

PetroleumBRUNEI — the national petroleum authority responsible for policy, regulation, and management of Brunei's oil and gas resources. AMBD (Autoriti Monetari Brunei Darussalam) — Brunei's central bank and financial regulator. Department of Energy — under the Prime Minister's Office, coordinates energy policy and the Wawasan 2035 energy diversification strategy.

Note: Brunei is an absolute monarchy (Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah). There is no elected legislature — the Legislative Council (Majlis Mesyuarat Negara) is appointed and has advisory functions only. Civic engagement operates through petition to the Sultan and through ASEAN and international multilateral channels. ASEAN — Brunei is a founding member. ASEAN's Framework for Circular Economy and declaration on 기후변화 provide regional engagement mechanisms.

BO.s: Resource-dependent 경제 ($54.9B GDP) with significant natural gas reserves (gas=1.85% rents) and 광업 (min=5.91%, lithium in Salar de Uyuni — world's largest deposit). 농업 GDP의 8.8% GDP with soy expansion driving 삼림 벌채. Gas production declining from mature fields.

sectors.w: Bolivia holds the world's largest lithium reserves in the Salar de Uyuni. Lithium extraction uses massive water volumes in arid regions. ...할 때 water consumption and brine chemistry data are published alongside production revenue, W가 계산 가능해진다.
bp: Require community benefit agreements and water rights guarantees for all lithium extraction. ...할 때 indigenous communities (who hold constitutional land rights under Bolivia's 2009 Constitution) can negotiate fair terms, ...에 대한 중복 이익이 extraction without consent 약화된다.
Mandatory publication of water extraction volumes, brine chemistry changes, and downstream ecosystem data per lithium operation. Making 환경 costs visible makes W computable from production data.

부분적으로 파괴 가능. Lithium brine extraction inherently alters aquifer chemistry and reduces water availability in arid ecosystems. However, direct lithium extraction (DLE) technology can significantly reduce water consumption compared to evaporation ponds.

i: AJAM (Autoridad Jurisdiccional Administrativa Minera) — the 광업 administrative authority responsible for granting and overseeing 광업 concessions. Defensoría del Pueblo — Bolivia's human rights ombudsman, responsible for investigating complaints about government actions affecting fundamental rights, including indigenous and 환경 rights. Bolivia's 2009 Constitution (Article 30) — guarantees indigenous peoples' right to prior consultation on resource extraction in their territories.

YLB (Yacimientos de Litio Bolivianos) — Bolivia's state lithium company, managing lithium industrialization in the Salar de Uyuni. COMIBOL (Corporación Minera de Bolivia) — the state 광업 corporation overseeing Bolivia's 광업 sector. YPFB (Yacimientos Petrolíferos Fiscales Bolivianos) — the state hydrocarbons company.

Ministerio de Minería y Metalurgia — Bolivia's 광업 ministry, responsible for 광업 policy and regulation. MMAyA (Ministerio de Medio Ambiente y Agua) — the environment and water ministry, overseeing 환경 policy and water resource management. SENARECOM (Servicio Nacional de Registro y Control de la Comercialización de Minerales y Metales) — the national service for mineral commerce registration and control.

Asamblea Legislativa Plurinacional (Plurinational Legislative Assembly) — Bolivia's bicameral parliament consisting of the Cámara de Diputados (130 members) and the Cámara de Senadores (36 members). Bolivia's 2009 Constitution establishes a 'plurinational' state recognizing indigenous rights and communitarian 민주주의. ILO Convention 169 — Bolivia has ratified ILO Convention 169 on Indigenous and Tribal Peoples, requiring free, prior, and informed consultation on resource extraction in indigenous territories.

BS.s: Small island 경제 ($15.8B GDP) dominated by tourism and financial services (77.2% services). Extreme climate vulnerability — low-lying islands face existential risk from sea level rise and intensifying hurricanes (Hurricane Dorian, 2019, caused $3.4B damage — 25% of GDP).

sectors.w: The Bahamas' offshore financial center enables capital flows that may avoid tax obligations elsewhere. ...할 때 beneficial ownership information and tax 투명성 data are published, the system welfare impact of offshore finance becomes computable.
bp: 이행하다 full beneficial ownership 투명성 in line with FATF recommendations. ...할 때 the competitive advantage of Bahamian financial secrecy is eliminated through 투명성, the 중복 이익 between offshore service providers and tax-avoiding clients 약화된다.

Publish aggregate data on capital flows, tax rulings, and beneficial ownership through enhanced OECD Common Reporting Standard participation. Making tax revenue impacts visible to source jurisdictions makes W computable.
부분적으로 파괴 가능. Financial intermediation can relocate — but the Bahamas' geographic proximity to the US and established legal infrastructure create switching costs that give regulation leverage.

i: FIU (Financial Intelligence Unit of The Bahamas) — the agency responsible for receiving, analyzing, and disseminating suspicious transaction reports. The FIU works with law enforcement to combat money laundering and terrorist financing. Securities Commission of The Bahamas — regulates the investment funds industry and securities market. Central Bank of The Bahamas — the prudential regulator of banks and trust companies.

The Bahamas is home to approximately 200 licensed banks and trust companies. Major international banks maintain operations in Nassau. The Bahamas 금융 서비스 Board (BFSB) promotes the financial services sector and coordinates industry standards.

Central Bank of The Bahamas — the primary financial regulator and supervisor. The Compliance Commission — supervises non-bank financial service providers for anti-money laundering compliance. FATF (Financial Action Task Force) — The Bahamas has been subject to FATF enhanced monitoring and has implemented reforms to address identified deficiencies in its AML/CFT framework.

Parliament of The Bahamas — bicameral legislature consisting of the House of Assembly (39 members) and the Senate (16 appointed members). Citizens can contact their MP through the official government portal. The Bahamas is a Westminster-style 의회 민주주의. AOSIS (Alliance of Small Island States) — The Bahamas is an active member, advocating for climate action and sea level rise mitigation. CARICOM — provides regional coordination on financial regulation.

BW.s: Upper-middle-income 경제 ($19.4B GDP) with diamond 광업 historically driving growth (Debswana JV with De Beers). Services-dominant (63.5% GDP). One of Africa's most stable democracies. Diversification challenge as lab-grown diamonds threaten natural diamond demand.

sectors.w: Lab-grown diamonds are disrupting the natural diamond market (30%+ of gem market by volume). As synthetic alternatives become indistinguishable and cheaper, the 중복 이익 between Debswana and consumers in natural diamond premium 약화된다 — Botswana must diversify.
bp: Accelerate economic diversification using Pula Fund sovereign wealth. ...할 때 non-diamond revenue streams (tech, tourism, beef) exceed declining diamond revenues, ...에 대한 중복 이익이 continued diamond dependence breaks naturally.
Publish full lifecycle 환경 data (water use, land disturbance, energy consumption) per carat for all 광업 operations. Making 환경 costs visible makes W computable from diamond production data.

부분적으로 파괴 가능. Open-pit diamond 광업 inherently disturbs landscapes. However, Botswana's mines are in relatively low-biodiversity Kalahari terrain, and rehabilitation programs can reduce long-term 시스템 의존성.
a: Report 환경 violations at mine sites to DEA (Department of Environmental Affairs)

Advocate for Kimberley Process reform to include 환경 and social standards

i: DEA (Department of Environmental Affairs) — under the Ministry of Environment and Tourism, responsible for 환경 policy, impact assessment, and monitoring. Department of Mines — under the Ministry of Minerals and Energy, responsible for 광업 safety regulation, licensing, and compliance monitoring. Office of the Ombudsman — investigates complaints against government agencies and public officials.

Debswana Diamond Company — a 50/50 joint venture between the Government of Botswana and De Beers Group, operating the Jwaneng and Orapa mines. Debswana is Botswana's largest private employer and the largest contributor to government revenue. De Beers Group (Anglo American subsidiary) — the global diamond company partnering with Botswana. The 2023 renegotiation of the Debswana agreement gave Botswana a greater share of rough diamond sales.

Ministry of Minerals and Energy — oversees 광업 policy and the regulatory framework under the Mines and Minerals Act. BURS (Botswana Unified Revenue Service) — the tax authority responsible for collecting mineral royalties and taxes. Pula Fund — Botswana's sovereign wealth fund (managed by the Bank of Botswana), designed to save diamond revenues for future generations.

National Assembly of Botswana — unicameral parliament with 65 members (57 elected, 8 specially elected). Citizens can contact their MPs through the 의회 website. Botswana is one of Africa's most stable multiparty democracies, with peaceful transfers of power since independence in 1966. The Ntlo ya Dikgosi (House of Chiefs) — an advisory body representing traditional leaders, with input on matters affecting tribal territories including 광업.

BY.s: State-controlled 경제 ($76B GDP) with significant manufacturing (20.3% GDP). Potash 광업 (Belaruskali is one of the world's largest producers). Oil refining (Naftan, Mozyr) processes Russian crude. Authoritarian governance under Lukashenko since 1994.

sectors.w: Belarus is the world's third-largest potash producer (Belaruskali). Potash 광업 generates massive salt waste (solukhards) that contaminate groundwater and rivers. ...할 때 downstream water contamination data is published alongside potash production revenue, W가 계산 가능해진다.
bp: International 제재 (EU, US, UK since 2021) have already weakened the 중복 이익 by restricting Belaruskali's market access. ...할 때 sanctioned export revenues fall below 환경 remediation costs, the economic case for continued expansion breaks.

Satellite-based 환경 monitoring (salt waste accumulation, water contamination plumes) published by international organizations. Making 환경 damage visible from space bypasses domestic data suppression and makes W computable.
부분적으로 파괴 가능. Potash 광업 inherently generates salt waste. However, solution 광업 and backfill techniques can reduce surface waste accumulation and groundwater contamination.

i: Note: Belarus under Lukashenko is rated 'Not Free' by Freedom House. Independent media, 시민 사회, and trade unions face severe repression. There is no effective 내부고발자 protection. After the 2020 protests, thousands were imprisoned and major independent organizations were forcibly liquidated. Safe reporting channels include the Belarusian diaspora network, international trade union federations (IndustriALL Global Union), and international 환경 organizations.

Belaruskali — Belarus's 국영 potash company, one of the world's largest potash producers. Belaruskali operates six mines near Soligorsk. The company has been under EU, US, and UK 제재 since 2021 following the forced diversion of Ryanair Flight 4978 and repression of democratic opposition. BPC (Belarusian Potash Company) — the former exclusive export trading arm of Belaruskali (sanctioned). Competitors include Nutrien (Canada), Mosaic (US), and K+S (Germany).

Note: Domestic regulatory institutions (Ministry of Natural Resources and Environmental Protection) are not independent under 권위주의 governance. International mechanisms include: UNECE (United Nations Economic Commission for Europe) — Belarus is party to the Espoo Convention and the Convention on the Protection and Use of Transboundary Watercourses. EU 제재 framework — Council Regulation (EU) restricting trade with Belaruskali and associated entities.

Note: Belarus has no functioning democratic legislature. The National Assembly (Natsionalnoye Sobranie) is not freely elected. International engagement channels: EU Eastern Partnership framework, OSCE (Organization for Security and Co-operation in Europe), United Nations Human Rights Council Universal Periodic Review. The Belarusian democratic opposition in exile (led by Sviatlana Tsikhanouskaya) maintains a platform including 환경 governance reform.

CD.s: Resource-rich but impoverished 경제 ($71B GDP). Mining dominates (28.81% of GDP) — world's largest cobalt producer (70%+ global supply), significant copper, coltan, and tin. Conflict minerals remain a governance challenge. Agricultural employment is 60%+ but only 9.6% of GDP.

sectors.w: DRC's cobalt is essential for EV batteries — downstream manufacturers (Tesla, Samsung SDI, CATL) face increasing ESG pressure. ...할 때 supply chain traceability makes mine-level 환경 and labor conditions visible to end consumers, W가 계산 가능해진다 from cobalt prices.
bp: Mandatory supply chain due diligence (EU Battery Regulation, US Dodd-Frank Section 1502) that requires full traceability to mine site. ...할 때 artisanal miners must meet labor and 환경 standards to access global markets, ...에 대한 중복 이익이 unregulated extraction 약화된다.

Blockchain or RMI-style traceability systems linking 광산별 환경 and labor data to each cobalt lot. The EU Battery Regulation (effective 2027) will require carbon footprint declarations and due diligence — making W computable per unit of cobalt.
부분적으로 파괴 가능. Mining inherently disturbs landscapes and generates waste. However, formalized artisanal 광업 with proper safety and 환경 management can reduce 시스템 의존성 compared to unregulated extraction.

Document child labor or unsafe working conditions — report through responsible sourcing audit channels (RMI, LBMA)

i: CEEC (Centre d'Évaluation, d'Expertise et de Certification des substances minérales précieuses et semi-précieuses) — the DRC government agency responsible for evaluating, certifying, and controlling mineral exports. SAEMAPE (Service d'Assistance et d'Encadrement du Small Scale Mining) — the agency responsible for regulating artisanal and small-scale 광업. IPIS (International Peace Information Service) — an independent research institute mapping 광업 sites, armed group presence, and supply chain risks in the DRC. Global Witness — international NGO investigating the links between natural resource exploitation and conflict.

Glencore — operates Mutanda and Katanga Mining (KCC) copper-cobalt mines, among the world's largest. CMOC (China Molybdenum Co.) — operates Tenke Fungurume, one of the world's largest copper-cobalt mines. Entreprise Générale du Cobalt (EGC) — state-backed enterprise intended to monopolize artisanal cobalt purchasing (established 2019). RMI (Responsible Minerals Initiative) — industry-led supply chain due diligence program used by electronics and automotive manufacturers.

Ministry of Mines — oversees 광업 policy, licensing, and the 2018 Mining Code (Code Minier), which increased royalty rates and introduced strategic substance classification for cobalt. CTCPM (Cellule Technique de Coordination et de Planification Minière) — the technical coordination unit for 광업 sector planning. ITSCI (ITRI Tin Supply Chain Initiative) — a traceability program for tin, tantalum, and tungsten (3T) minerals from the DRC.

Parlement de la République démocratique du Congo — bicameral legislature consisting of the Assemblée nationale (500 members) and the Sénat (108 members). The DRC is a semi-presidential republic but governance capacity is limited outside major urban centers. EU Battery Regulation (2023/1542) — effective 2027, requires carbon footprint declarations, recycled content minimums, and supply chain due diligence for batteries containing cobalt. OECD Due Diligence Guidance for Responsible Supply Chains of Minerals from Conflict-Affected and High-Risk Areas — the international standard for mineral supply chain responsibility.

CG.s: Oil-dependent 경제 ($15.7B GDP) with 34.74% combined oil and gas rents — one of Sub-Saharan Africa's highest. TotalEnergies and Eni are major operators. Governance challenges with long-ruling president (Sassou Nguesso since 1997). Significant tropical forest cover.

sectors.w: Congo-Brazzaville's oil production is in long-term decline from mature offshore fields. As extraction costs rise and reserves deplete, the 중복 이익 between international operators and government revenue 약화된다 — diversification becomes necessary.
bp: Redirect oil revenues to economic diversification (forestry, agriculture, services) through transparent budget allocation. ...할 때 non-oil sectors generate more employment and revenue, ...에 대한 중복 이익이 continued extraction from depleting fields breaks.
이행하다 EITI-standard per-block revenue and 배출 disclosure. Congo joined EITI in 2004 — extending to 환경 data makes W computable per production unit.
파괴 불가능 by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.

i: Ministère de l'Environnement, du Développement Durable et du Bassin du Congo — the environment ministry. SNPC (Société Nationale des Pétroles du Congo) — the national oil company. EITI Congo — the Republic of Congo has been an EITI member since 2004. Note: Whistleblower protections are minimal under current governance.

TotalEnergies — the largest oil operator in Congo-Brazzaville, operating the Moho Nord deepwater project and other fields. Eni — operates significant offshore concessions. SNPC (Société Nationale des Pétroles du Congo) — the national oil company holding state equity in all production sharing contracts.

Ministère des Hydrocarbures — oversees oil and gas policy and licensing. BEAC (Banque des États de l'Afrique Centrale) — the central bank of the CEMAC monetary zone (shared with Cameroon, CAR, Chad, Equatorial Guinea, Gabon). EITI Congo — publishes annual reports on extractive industry revenues.

Note: Congo-Brazzaville under President Sassou Nguesso is rated 'Not Free' by Freedom House. The Assemblée nationale (151 members) and Sénat (72 members) do not function as independent democratic institutions. International engagement channels: CEMAC (Communauté Économique et Monétaire de l'Afrique Centrale), African Union, CAFI (Central African Forest Initiative) — a partnership between Central African countries and donors to protect tropical forests.

CI.s: Largest 경제 in francophone West Africa ($87.1B GDP). World's largest cocoa producer (~45% of global supply). Agriculture is 15.9% of GDP with cocoa, coffee, and palm oil as main cash crops. Growing oil production (oil=0.7%). Rapid economic growth (~7% annual average).

sectors.w: Côte d'Ivoire's cocoa monoculture has driven 80%+ of original forest loss. EU Deforestation Regulation (EUDR) will require 삼림 벌채-free supply chains — when per-farm 삼림 벌채 data is linked to cocoa transactions, W가 계산 가능해진다.
bp: EU Deforestation Regulation compliance forces traceability to farm level. ...할 때 cocoa buyers must verify zero-삼림 벌채, the 중복 이익 between traders and smallholders in clearing forest for new plantations breaks — agroforestry becomes the viable model.

Mandatory per-farm 환경 data (tree cover, soil carbon, water use) linked to cocoa lot traceability. The Cocoa and Forests Initiative already creates a framework — extending to mandatory data makes W computable from cocoa prices.

부분적으로 파괴 가능. Cocoa monoculture inherently reduces biodiversity compared to native forest. However, cocoa agroforestry systems can maintain significant tree cover and biodiversity — Côte d'Ivoire's National REDD+ Strategy includes agroforestry targets.

Document child labor in cocoa production — report to CNS (Comité National de Surveillance des actions de lutte contre le travail des enfants)

i: SODEFOR (Société de Développement des Forêts) — the state forestry company responsible for managing classified forests, many of which have been illegally cleared for cocoa. OIPR (Office Ivoirien des Parcs et Réserves) — manages national parks and protected areas. CNS (Comité National de Surveillance) — the national committee monitoring child labor in cocoa, coordinated with the International Cocoa Initiative (ICI). ANADER — the national rural development support agency providing agricultural extension services.

Major cocoa traders operating in Côte d'Ivoire: Cargill, Barry Callebaut, Olam International, Sucden. These companies intermediate the majority of Ivorian cocoa exports. Cocoa and Forests Initiative (CFI) — a public-private partnership between cocoa-producing countries (Côte d'Ivoire, Ghana) and leading chocolate companies to end 삼림 벌채 in cocoa supply chains. The Conseil du Café-Cacao (CCC) — the Ivorian cocoa board that sets farmgate prices and regulates the sector.

CCC (Conseil du Café-Cacao) — the government body regulating Côte d'Ivoire's cocoa and coffee sectors, setting minimum farmgate prices, managing quality standards, and overseeing exports. MINEDD (Ministère de l'Environnement et du Développement Durable) — the environment ministry responsible for forest protection, climate policy, and 환경 regulation.

Assemblée nationale de Côte d'Ivoire — unicameral parliament with 255 members. Citizens can contact deputies through the official Assembly website. Sénat — the upper house (99 members, established 2020). ICCO (International Cocoa Organization) — the intergovernmental body coordinating global cocoa policy, headquartered in Abidjan. EU Deforestation Regulation (EUDR, 2023/1115) — requires EU importers to verify that commodities including cocoa are not linked to 삼림 벌채 after December 2020.

CL.s: Upper-middle-income 경제 ($330.3B GDP) with massive 광업 sector (16.23% of GDP). World's largest copper producer (~27% of global supply) and second-largest lithium producer. Strong institutions and democratic governance. Renewable energy leader in Latin America (solar in Atacama).

sectors.w: Chile's copper and lithium 광업 depletes water resources in the Atacama Desert (one of Earth's driest regions). ...할 때 광산별 water consumption data is published alongside production revenue, W가 계산 가능해진다 from 광업 output data.
bp: 이행하다 water pricing that reflects true scarcity in 광업 regions. ...할 때 miners pay the real cost of water extraction in hyper-arid zones, ...에 대한 중복 이익이 water-intensive extraction methods 약화된다 — desalination and dry processing become economically preferred.

Mandatory 광산별 water balance, tailings chemistry, and 배출 disclosure. Chile's SMA (Superintendencia del Medio Ambiente) already collects 환경 data — making it publicly accessible per mine makes W computable.
부분적으로 파괴 가능. Mining inherently disturbs landscapes. However, dry processing, desalination, and tailings reprocessing can significantly reduce water dependence and waste generation.

i: SMA (Superintendencia del Medio Ambiente) — Chile's 환경 enforcement agency, responsible for monitoring compliance with 환경 permits (RCA — Resolución de Calificación Ambiental). SMA can impose fines up to $8.2M and order facility closure. DGA (Dirección General de Aguas) — the national water authority managing water rights and monitoring extraction. SEA (Servicio de Evaluación Ambiental) — conducts 환경 impact assessments for 광업 projects.

CODELCO (Corporación Nacional del Cobre) — Chile's 국영 copper company and the world's largest copper producer. CODELCO operates Chuquicamata, El Teniente, and other major mines. SQM (Sociedad Química y Minera de Chile) — one of the world's largest lithium producers, operating in the Salar de Atacama. Albemarle — US company operating lithium extraction in the Salar de Atacama alongside SQM. BHP, Anglo American — major international copper 광업 companies operating in Chile.

Sernageomin (Servicio Nacional de Geología y Minería) — the national geological and 광업 service, responsible for mine safety, tailings dam oversight, and geological surveys. Chile has over 740 tailings facilities, the largest inventory in the world. Ministry of Mining — oversees 광업 policy including the 2023 National Lithium Strategy, which established state-led lithium development through public-private partnerships.

Congreso Nacional de Chile — bicameral legislature consisting of the Cámara de Diputadas y Diputados (155 members) and the Senado (50 members). Citizens can contact representatives through camara.cl and senado.cl. Chile is an OECD member with strong democratic institutions. Escazú Agreement — the Regional Agreement on Access to Information, Public Participation and Justice in Environmental Matters in Latin America and the Caribbean. Chile signed but has not ratified.

CM.s: Central African 경제 ($53.3B GDP) with oil production (2.94% combined rents) and significant agriculture (18.5% GDP — cocoa, palm oil, timber). Forest cover declining due to logging and agricultural expansion. Governance challenges and Anglophone crisis.

sectors.w: Cameroon's oil production is declining from mature fields. As extraction costs rise, the 중복 이익 between operators (Perenco, Addax) and government revenue 약화된다 — diversification to sustainable forestry and agriculture becomes economically rational.
bp: Redirect declining oil revenues to sustainable forest management and agricultural diversification. Cameroon's forests are a carbon sink worth more standing than cleared. ...할 때 REDD+ payments exceed marginal oil revenue, ...에 대한 중복 이익이 continued extraction breaks.
이행하다 EITI-standard per-block 환경 disclosure. Cameroon's EITI membership provides the framework — extending to 환경 data makes W computable.

i: MINEPDED (Ministère de l'Environnement, de la Protection de la Nature et du Développement Durable) — Cameroon's environment ministry. SNH (Société Nationale des Hydrocarbures) — the national hydrocarbons company managing state interests in oil and gas. EITI Cameroon — member since 2007. Note: Whistleblower protections are limited.

Perenco — the largest oil producer in Cameroon. Addax Petroleum (Sinopec subsidiary) — operates offshore concessions. SNH manages the state's petroleum interests and holds equity in production sharing contracts.

MINMIDT (Ministère des Mines, de l'Industrie et du Développement Technologique) — oversees 광업 and industrial policy. CONAC (Commission Nationale Anti-Corruption) — Cameroon's anti-corruption commission.

Assemblée nationale du Cameroun — 180 members. Sénat — 100 members. Note: Cameroon's democratic institutions are constrained under long-ruling President Paul Biya (in power since 1982). International channels: CEMAC, African Union, CAFI.

CO.s: Major Latin American 경제 ($418.8B GDP) with significant oil (3.42% rents) and coal (0.73% rents) production. Ecopetrol is the largest company. Biodiversity hotspot (most biodiverse country per square kilometer). 2016 peace agreement with FARC opened new governance challenges in former conflict zones.

sectors.w: Colombia's current administration (Petro, 2022-) has announced a policy of no new oil exploration licenses. If maintained, ...에 대한 중복 이익이 fossil fuel expansion breaks as existing fields deplete. Colombia's renewable potential (hydro, solar, wind) provides viable alternatives.
bp: Maintain and implement the no-new-exploration-licenses policy while investing transition revenues in 재생에너지. ...할 때 energy investment flows shift from fossil to renewable, ...에 대한 중복 이익이 continued exploration breaks.

Mandatory 유전별 배출 disclosure for all Ecopetrol and international operator fields. Colombia's ANH (Agencia Nacional de Hidrocarburos) already collects production data — extending to 배출 makes W computable.
파괴 불가능 by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.

i: ANLA (Autoridad Nacional de Licencias Ambientales) — Colombia's 환경 licensing authority, responsible for 환경 impact assessment and compliance monitoring for extractive projects. Defensoría del Pueblo — the national human rights ombudsman. Contraloría General de la República — the national audit office overseeing public resource management including oil revenues.

Ecopetrol — Colombia's largest company (88.5% 국영), responsible for most domestic oil production. Ecopetrol has announced energy transition targets but continues significant upstream investment. ANH (Agencia Nacional de Hidrocarburos) — manages exploration and production contracts.

MinAmbiente (Ministerio de Ambiente y Desarrollo Sostenible) — Colombia's environment ministry. CREG (Comisión de Regulación de Energía y Gas) — the 에너지 규제기관y commission. CAR (Corporaciones Autónomas Regionales) — regional 환경 authorities.

Congreso de la República de Colombia — bicameral legislature: Cámara de Representantes (188 members) and Senado (108 members). Contact through camara.gov.co and senado.gov.co. Colombia is an OECD member (2020) with strong democratic institutions. The 2016 Peace Agreement (Acuerdo de Paz) created new governance frameworks for former conflict zones that intersect with extractive industry regulation.

CR.s: Upper-middle-income 경제 ($95.4B GDP) with 98%+ renewable electricity generation. No military since 1948. Services-dominant (68.8% GDP). Leader in ecotourism and 환경 policy. Low direct fossil fuel extraction. Main PST exposure through agricultural monoculture (pineapple, banana, palm oil).

sectors.w: Costa Rica's pineapple and banana monoculture uses intensive pesticide application that contaminates waterways. ...할 때 per-farm pesticide runoff and water contamination data is published alongside export revenue, W가 계산 가능해진다.
bp: 개혁하다 agricultural export incentives to reward biodiversity-positive farming. ...할 때 export tax benefits require 환경 certification, the 중복 이익 between monoculture producers and exporters in chemical-intensive production 약화된다.

Mandatory per-farm water quality monitoring downstream of monoculture operations. Costa Rica's AyA (water utility) and SINAC already collect water data — linking to agricultural output makes W computable.
부분적으로 파괴 가능. Tropical monoculture inherently reduces biodiversity. However, Costa Rica's Payment for Environmental Services (PSA) program demonstrates viable alternatives.

i: SFE (Servicio Fitosanitario del Estado) — the state phytosanitary service regulating pesticide use. MINAE (Ministerio de Ambiente y Energía) — the environment and energy ministry. AyA (Instituto Costarricense de Acueductos y Alcantarillados) — the national water utility. SETENA (Secretaría Técnica Nacional Ambiental) — conducts 환경 impact assessments.

Costa Rica hosts operations of major fruit multinationals: Del Monte, Dole, Chiquita. CORBANA (Corporación Bananera Nacional) — the national banana corporation coordinating the sector. CANAPEP (Cámara Nacional de Productores y Exportadores de Piña) — the pineapple producers' association.

MAG (Ministerio de Agricultura y Ganadería) — Costa Rica's agriculture ministry. The PSA (Pago por Servicios Ambientales) program — Costa Rica's pioneering payment for 환경 services program, which pays landowners to maintain forest cover, funded partly by a fuel tax.

Asamblea Legislativa de Costa Rica — unicameral parliament with 57 members. Citizens can contact representatives through asamblea.go.cr. Costa Rica is one of Latin America's strongest democracies and a global 환경 leader. The Escazú Agreement — Costa Rica was one of the first signatories of this regional agreement on 환경 access rights.

CU.s: Command 경제 ($107.4B GDP, though estimates vary due to dual currency system). Services-dominant (73.5% GDP, driven by healthcare, education, tourism). Minimal resource extraction. Economic crisis exacerbated by US embargo and collapse of Venezuelan oil subsidies.

sectors.w: Cuba's sugar industry collapsed after Soviet subsidies ended, but remaining agriculture (state farms, cooperatives) relies on monoculture patterns. Cuba's organic farming necessity (post-Soviet fertilizer shortage) demonstrates alternative models. Making soil health data visible breaks system independence.
bp: Expand the urban agriculture and organoponics model that Cuba developed out of necessity. ...할 때 organic production meets domestic food needs more reliably than import-dependent monoculture, ...에 대한 중복 이익이 conventional agriculture breaks.
Publish soil health, water quality, and biodiversity data from Cuba's extensive agricultural research system. Cuba's scientific institutions (INIFAT, ICA) generate data that could make W computable.
부분적으로 파괴 가능. Cuba's experience with organic farming post-1991 shows that 시스템 의존성 on chemical inputs can be dramatically reduced through agroecological methods.

i: Note: Cuba is a 일당 국가. Independent 시민 사회, media, and labor organizations are not permitted. Reporting channels operate within state structures. CTC (Central de Trabajadores de Cuba) — the state-controlled trade union federation. INIFAT (Instituto Nacional de Investigaciones Fundamentales en Agricultura Tropical) — the national agricultural research institute. CITMA (Ministerio de Ciencia, Tecnología y Medio Ambiente) — the science and environment ministry.

AZCUBA (Empresa Azucarera) — Cuba's state sugar enterprise. State agricultural enterprises and UBPCs (Unidades Básicas de Producción Cooperativa) — cooperatives managing most agricultural production. Cuba's urban agriculture program is one of the world's most successful, producing significant food in cities through organoponics.

MINAG (Ministerio de la Agricultura) — Cuba's agriculture ministry. CITMA — oversees 환경 policy and scientific research. The Asociación Nacional de Agricultores Pequeños (ANAP) — the national small farmers' association, which has been a key partner in agroecological transition.

Note: Cuba's Asamblea Nacional del Poder Popular (605 members) is the legislature but functions within a one-party system. International engagement channels: FAO (Cuba has received recognition for reducing hunger), CELAC (Community of Latin American and Caribbean States), NAM (Non-Aligned Movement). Cuba's agroecological experience is studied globally as a model for post-petrochemical agriculture.

CY.s: Small EU island 경제 ($37.6B GDP) with dominant services sector (76.5% GDP, driven by financial services, shipping, and tourism). No significant resource extraction. Post-2013 banking crisis reforms strengthened financial regulation. Division between Republic of Cyprus and northern territory.

sectors.w: Cyprus's financial services sector has historically facilitated capital flows from Russia and other jurisdictions. EU AML directives and beneficial ownership 투명성 requirements make the welfare impact of opaque financial intermediation computable.
bp: Full implementation of EU Anti-Money Laundering Directives and beneficial ownership 투명성. ...할 때 financial secrecy is eliminated, the 중복 이익 between Cypriot service providers and clients seeking opacity 약화된다.

Mandatory publication of Country-by-Country Reporting (CbCR) data for entities registered in Cyprus. EU ATAD and DAC6 frameworks provide the mechanism — making tax planning structures visible breaks system independence.
부분적으로 파괴 가능. Financial intermediation can relocate — but Cyprus's EU 회원국ship, common law heritage, and timezone advantages create switching costs.

Cyprus transposed the EU Whistleblower Directive in 2022.

Cyprus hosts approximately 40 licensed banks and numerous investment firms. The Cyprus Investment Firms sector manages significant cross-border capital flows. CIFA (Cyprus Investment Funds Association) — the industry association representing investment fund managers.

CySEC — responsible for securities regulation and investment firm supervision. CBC (Central Bank of Cyprus) — conducts banking supervision and AML compliance monitoring. ICPAC — the professional body for accountants and auditors in Cyprus.

Βουλή των Αντιπροσώπων (House of Representatives) — unicameral parliament with 56 elected members (80 seats including 24 reserved for Turkish Cypriot community). Contact through parliament.cy. Cyprus is an EU 회원국 with robust democratic institutions. Moneyval (Council of Europe's Committee of Experts on the Evaluation of Anti-Money Laundering Measures) evaluates Cyprus's AML framework.

CZ.s: Central European industrial 경제 ($347B GDP) with strong manufacturing (19.9% GDP, automotive-dominated). Small coal sector (lignite in northern Bohemia). EU 회원국. Significant nuclear energy capacity (Dukovany, Temelín). 서비스업 GDP의 60.2% GDP.

sectors.w: Czechia's lignite 광업 is concentrated in the Ústí nad Labem region (Sokolovská uhelná, Severní energetická). EU ETS carbon pricing makes Czech lignite uncompetitive — the 중복 이익 between mine operators and local employment breaks when carbon costs exceed revenue.
bp: Align with EU ETS carbon pricing and eliminate free allowances for Czech lignite power. ...할 때 plants pay the full carbon price, lignite electricity becomes more expensive than alternatives and the 중복 이익 breaks.
Mandatory 발전소별 배출 and health-impact reporting. Czech lignite plants cause significant air pollution — publishing health costs per MWh makes W computable.
파괴 불가능. Lignite combustion releases CO₂ and pollutants by physical process.

i: ČIŽP (Česká inspekce životního prostředí / Czech Environmental Inspectorate) — the enforcement agency for 환경 law, conducting inspections and imposing 제재. ČEZ Group — Czechia's largest energy company (70% 국영), operating coal, nuclear, and renewable power plants. Sev.en Energy — the largest private lignite 광업 and power company. Sokolovská uhelná — operates lignite mines in the Sokolov basin.

ERÚ (Energetický regulační úřad / Energy Regulatory Office) — the independent 에너지 규제기관. Czech Coal Commission (Uhelná komise) — established to recommend a coal phase-out date. Recommended 2033, but implementation timelines remain debated.

Parlament České republiky — bicameral: Poslanecká sněmovna (200 members) and Senát (81 members). Contact through psp.cz and senat.cz. EU Just Transition Fund — Czechia is eligible for significant funding for coal-dependent regions.

DK.s: Advanced Nordic 경제 ($424.5B GDP) with global leadership in wind energy (Vestas, Ørsted). Intensive livestock agriculture (28M pigs for 5.8M people) creates significant nitrogen pollution. 서비스업 GDP의 63.5% GDP. World's most ambitious climate law (70% reduction by 2030).

sectors.w: Denmark's intensive pig farming generates nitrogen pollution visible in marine dead zones (inner Danish waters, Baltic Sea). EU Nitrates Directive compliance data makes W computable from farm-level nitrogen balance sheets.
bp: 개혁하다 agricultural subsidies to penalize nitrogen surplus per hectare. ...할 때 the 중복 이익 between livestock producers and feed importers is broken by internalizing the nitrogen externality, intensive livestock becomes unprofitable without subsidy.

Mandatory farm-level nitrogen and phosphorus balance disclosure. Denmark already has one of Europe's most detailed agricultural data systems — extending to mandatory public disclosure makes W computable.

부분적으로 파괴 가능. Intensive livestock inherently concentrates nutrients beyond ecosystem absorption. However, Denmark's leadership in biogas from manure demonstrates 시스템 의존성 can be reduced through circular nutrient management.

Danish Crown — one of Europe's largest meat processors (farmer cooperative). Arla Foods — multinational dairy cooperative headquartered in Denmark. DLG Group — major agricultural input cooperative.

Miljøministeriet (Ministry of Environment) — 환경 policy including agricultural regulation. Landbrugsstyrelsen (Danish Agricultural Agency) — administers CAP payments. The 2021 green agriculture agreement is one of Europe's most ambitious.

DO.s: Caribbean's largest 경제 ($124.3B GDP) with tourism, manufacturing (free trade zones), and 광업 (2.04% GDP — Pueblo Viejo gold mine, one of the world's largest). 서비스업 GDP의 59.8% GDP. Climate vulnerable — hurricanes and sea level rise.

sectors.w: Pueblo Viejo gold mine (Barrick Gold) generates cyanide and mercury contamination risks. ...할 때 광산별 water quality and health data is published alongside production revenue, W가 계산 가능해진다.
bp: Mandate 환경 bonds that fully internalize mine closure and remediation costs. ...할 때 광업 companies pre-fund cleanup, ...에 대한 중복 이익이 extraction without 환경 accountability 약화된다.
Mandatory water quality monitoring and health-impact disclosure per mine. Publishing downstream contamination data makes W computable from 광업 revenue.
부분적으로 파괴 가능. Open-pit 광업 disturbs landscapes. However, improved tailings management and water treatment can reduce 시스템 의존성.
